title: Shinjis logic
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's logic             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sun Jul 27, 2014 9:00 am What is usually believed about Shinji is he so worthless that he can't commit suicide, but this is something I find very controversial becuase if someone wants to kill themselves they'll have no problem to find a way to end their lives. Suicide is also that requires guts : - If someone wants to blown their heads with a shotgun it requires a lot courage to pull the trigger. And you need to shot the right place or else you'll survive and live the rest of your life with a disfigured face. - If someone wants to hang themselves it requires a lot of courage to kick the chair and let be hanged by the rope. And you have to get a good rope or else it will snap and you'll fall on the floor. - If someone wants to drown themselves it requires a lot of courage to try breathe underwater and stay the necessairly amount of time to drown. Does Shinji think he's so worthless to kill himself because he already tried and failed (which is hard to happen) or because he dosen't have the ballz to take his own life? What you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sun Jul 27, 2014 9:10 am Shini is not driven by logic. Shinji is driven by Anno's whim. Therefore he won't kill imself since he is Anno's chewtoy that is designed to be abused and now he have to learn to take it with a smile and enjoy it, since he won't be anything more

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sun Jul 27, 2014 9:21 am Stillborn wrote: Shini is not driven by logic. Shinji is driven by Anno's whim. Therefore he won't kill imself since he is Anno's chewtoy that is designed to be abused and now he have to learn to take it with a smile and enjoy it, since he won't be anything more

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:22 am Like most depressed people he probably doesn't really want to die. Shinji just wants to be 'happy' without yet having a true definition of happiness, more like. Or for life to be easy without having to go through any hardship or stress. He's not exactly suicidal at any point in the series, more just apathetic about his own life. Suicide does take ballz.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:29 am Shinji doesn't have enough will or conviction to kill himself. He's depressed, hates himself, and sometimes wants nothing more than to cease his continued existence, but he has no strong feelings one way or another and cannot bring himself to follow through with suicide. Shinji is only seen to be suicidal really once throughout the series, which is after Kaworu's death. Episode 04 suggests he was considering suicide by jumping off the cliff, but I still wouldn't consider him suicidal during that time, at least not seriously suicidal. The episode dealt with his character on a much larger level, displaying to us his normal tendency to run away from things when they get tough; the nod to the famous suicide spot can be interpreted as an extension of that theme. However, in that instance his consideration is simply that: a consideration; he was toying with the idea. His depression after episode 24 is when we see him truly suicidal and an actual danger to himself, and that ends up being the focus of each ending.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:31 am I thought it was made pretty clear that he's not suicidal, he just doesn't want to exist. Which is what the whole point of both endings were about, being able to exist, and liking yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 27, 2014 11:14 am To go through suicide you need to have a certain hate of yourself to muster the "courage" to harm yourself. Shinji at the point of EoE is too emotionally drained to muster the active hate that would let him follow through it.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jul 27, 2014 11:27 am Of times, the mentality of a suicide is a cheerful one. People report that family and friends that off themselves often seem upbeat prior to the act, are happy in settling their affairs and making preparations, because their suffering is almost over. Shinji does not feel worthy of that kind of happiness, and if it were all to end, he couldn't continue punishing himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sun Jul 27, 2014 2:11 pm Chuckman wrote: Shinji does not feel worthy of that kind of happiness, and if it were all to end, he couldn't continue punishing himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jul 27, 2014 5:02 pm Shinji is not a masochist. The problem with people in his situation is that he really thinks he deserves pain. He doesn't want it, but his world everything is seemingly his fault and he is worthless. He doesn't value himself, and thus doesn't take the steps necessary to take care of himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sun Jul 27, 2014 5:24 pm The sad thing is that in Q apparently his former comrades share the sentiment. He doesn't deserve death, but he deserves pain. If not physical then emotional.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sun Jul 27, 2014 6:57 pm He might feel like a piece of shit. But has he ever thought that his presence might annoy or unplease someone? If it haven't before... The only way to stop existing is death or trap himself inside EVA 01. But, he dosen't want to die nor stay forever inside EVA 01 (with his mother). What does he want? To someone cast a Vanishing Spell on him?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 27, 2014 7:06 pm ^ Don't expect people utterly depressed to follow logic. He does want to cease to exist so he won't have to feel pain anymore, but don't want to got through the motions to do it and commit suicide or trap himself inside EVA-01 (proof that he simply don't want to die and deep down does value his life a little), then comes Instrumentality and gives him exactly that: cessation of existence via the destruction of his individuality (and the rest of mankind with him), without having to pull a trigger and jump from a bridge. So he gives it a try, realize that finally he likes living and be an individual, and reject Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sun Jul 27, 2014 7:12 pm ^^ Judging by his "suicide by enemy soldier" intent in EoE that may be very close to true. He needs someone to kill him since he can't self terminate. ^ Instrumentality didn't gave him what he wanted actually. He wanted to cease to exist, yet he still had his consciousness additionaly burdened with other people. So this was not the disappearance he wanted. He didn't liked it since it failed to erase him.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sun Jul 27, 2014 8:37 pm ElMariachi wrote: then comes Instrumentality and gives him exactly that: cessation of existence via the destruction of his individuality (and the rest of mankind with him), without having to pull a trigger and jump from a bridge. So he gives it a try, realize that finally he likes living and be an individual, and reject Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 28, 2014 1:15 am Thomas68 wrote: He rejected Instrumentality becuase that would cost the rest of the planet to disappear together with him and he didn't wanted that. Instrumentality would be the perfect solution for him if it didn't involved innocent people.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jul 28, 2014 3:04 am In fact, it looks like that except Kaji and Misato, absolutely no one in the cast gave any consideration for mankind's continued existence, no wonder it all got to hell like it did! (which on a side-note is what make WILLE so original in the franchise: it's the first time we see an organization gathering all of those who do have consideration for mankind's continued existence)

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Jul 28, 2014 6:13 am Sachi wrote: His reasons for rejecting Instrumentality were personal, not because of the collateral damage he caused for other people with Instrumentality. Shinji chose to continue living as an individual among other individuals, despite the pain associated with ambiguous interactions and not being able to understand other people. He found the will to live and to continue searching for the "hope" and "love" that Kaworu and Rei had been talking to him about; something that was not on their list of arguments was the consideration for mankind's continued existence.

[]: Re: Shinji's logic             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Aug 01, 2014 9:09 pm Thomas68 wrote: What is usually believed about Shinji is he so worthless that he can't commit suicide, but this is something I find very controversial becuase if someone wants to kill themselves they'll have no problem to find a way to end their lives. Suicide is also that requires guts :

[]: Re: Shinji's logic             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sat Aug 02, 2014 2:37 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji discovers in the End of Evangelion that suicide, running away, is not the answer. His decision ends Complementation/Instrumentality and frees everyone, but as Sachi has pointed out it is first and foremost a personal decision based on the allegory of Third Impact as a suicide attempt which the entire series has been building towards. Shinji could not commit suicide at the beginning of the series, and only the events which follow finally push him over the edge.
summary: []: Shinji's logic             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sun Jul 27, 2014 9:00 am What is usually believed about Shinji is he so worthless that he can't commit suicide, but this is something I find very controversial becuase if someone wants to kill themselves they'll have no problem to find a way to end their lives. Suicide is also that requires guts : - If someone wants to blown their heads with a shotgun it requires a lot courage
